attempts.error:
Predicate argument has wrong type:
  atLocation lamp end_table: lamp is not a agent
  atLocation pillow armchair: pillow is not a agent
attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Pick up a pillow and turn on a lamp.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains two primary objects relevant to the instruction: a pillow and a lamp. The lamp is situated on a wooden end table, which is positioned next to a brown leather sofa. The lamp has a pink conical base and a rectangular shade with a green and black striped pattern. The lamp is currently off, as indicated by the absence of emitted light.

The pillow is placed on the seat of a brown armchair, which is located near the end table. The pillow has a decorative pattern with a maroon and beige geometric design. The armchair is upholstered in a smooth, brown material, matching the sofa.

Neither the lamp nor the pillow is classified as a receptacle in the domain, as they are not designed to hold other objects. The end table is a surface but is not defined as a receptacle in the domain file, so it cannot be used for “PutObjectInReceptacle” actions. The lamp is toggleable, as it can be turned on or off, and the pillow is a simple object that can be picked up.

The robot is not currently at any object’s location and must first navigate to either the lamp or the pillow to begin the task. The lamp is on the end table, which is adjacent to the sofa, and the pillow is on the armchair, which is in close proximity to the end table. The spatial layout suggests that the robot can reach either object with minimal movement.

No objects in the scene are currently being held by the agent, and no objects are in a receptacle. The lamp is not toggled on, and the pillow is not being held. The robot must first go to the pillow to pick it up, then go to the lamp to toggle it on. The end table and armchair serve as static surfaces for object placement but are not receptacles in the domain’s definition.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem pick_pillow_and_turn_on_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pillow - object
        lamp - object
        end_table - object
        armchair - object
        sofa - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot end_table) ; Assuming robot starts near the end table for efficiency
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        (not (holds robot pillow))
        (not (atLocation robot pillow))
        (not (atLocation robot lamp))
        ; Pillow is on the armchair
        (atLocation pillow armchair)
        ; Lamp is on the end table
        (atLocation lamp end_table)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot pillow)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem pick_pillow_and_turn_on_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pillow - object
        lamp - object
        end_table - object
        armchair - object
        sofa - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot end_table) ; Assuming robot starts near the end table for efficiency
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (not (isToggled lamp))
        (not (holds robot pillow))
        (not (atLocation robot pillow))
        (not (atLocation robot lamp))
        ; Pillow is on the armchair
        (atLocation pillow armchair)
        ; Lamp is on the end table
        (atLocation lamp end_table)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot pillow)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene contains a living room setting with a side table and a sofa. The following objects are present and relevant to the task:

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - "Pillow": A square cushion with a purple and beige geometric pattern, placed on the seat of a brown sofa. In the domain, this is classified as an "object" (not a receptacle).
   - "Lamp": A table lamp with a pink conical base and a rectangular green-and-black striped lampshade, placed on a dark brown wooden side table. The lamp is an "object" in the domain and is toggleable (can be turned on/off).
   - "Side Table": A dark brown wooden table with a single drawer. It is an "object" in the domain, but not a receptacle as per the domain definition (only microwave, fridge, sink are receptacles). It serves as a surface for the lamp and other items.
   - "Sofa": A brown upholstered sofa with armrests, supporting the pillow. It is an "object" and not a receptacle in this domain.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 Pillow
   - 1 Lamp
   - 1 Side Table
   - 1 Sofa

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The pillow is located on the sofa (specifically on its seat cushion).
   - The lamp is located on the side table.
   - The side table is positioned adjacent to the sofa, against a beige wall.
   - The lamp is not inside any receptacle; it is placed on the surface of the side table.
   - The sofa and side table are both on a light beige carpeted floor.

4. Current State of Objects (Relevant to Planning Task):
   - The lamp is currently off (not illuminated). Its state is “not isOn”.
   - The pillow is stationary and not held by any agent.
   - The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

5. Visual Properties for Distinguishing Objects:
   - The pillow has a distinctive purple and beige geometric pattern and is square-shaped.
   - The lamp has a pink base and a rectangular lampshade with green horizontal stripes and black borders.
   - The side table is dark brown wood with visible grain and a single drawer with a gold-colored handle.
   - The sofa is a solid brown color with a smooth, slightly shiny upholstery texture.

No receptacles (microwave, fridge, sink) are visible in the scene. The robot must first navigate to the sofa to pick up the pillow, then navigate to the side table to toggle the lamp on.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem pick_pillow_and_turn_on_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pillow - object
        lamp - object
        side_table - object
        sofa - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot side_table) ; Assuming robot starts near side table for simplicity, as no initial location is specified
        (inReceptacle pillow sofa) ; Pillow is on the sofa
        (inReceptacle lamp side_table) ; Lamp is on the side table
        (not (isOn lamp)) ; Lamp is currently off
        (not (holdsAny robot)) ; Robot is not holding anything
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot pillow)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
```

(gotolocation robot pillow)

final.problem:
(define (problem pick_pillow_and_turn_on_lamp)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pillow - object
        lamp - object
        side_table - object
        sofa - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot side_table) ; Assuming robot starts near side table for simplicity, as no initial location is specified
        (inReceptacle pillow sofa) ; Pillow is on the sofa
        (inReceptacle lamp side_table) ; Lamp is on the side table
        (not (isOn lamp)) ; Lamp is currently off
        (not (holdsAny robot)) ; Robot is not holding anything
    )
    (:goal (and
        (holds robot pillow)
        (isOn lamp)
    ))
)
